prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ARIMA', seasonality_champion='CWT_rf', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.94) and high seasonality variance (0.70), with early horizon specialists (CWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_catboost) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_svr, CWT_svr). ARIMA dominates trend tracking while CWT_rf leads in seasonality capture. The FT_* models perform poorly across all metrics and should be excluded. Given the high disagreement and variance signals, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ARIMA",
  "seasonality_champion": "CWT_rf",
  "overall_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "CWT_rf", "late": "DWT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["DWT_svr", "CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "DWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "catboost", "FT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_CWT_svr", "ONLY_DWT_svr", "CWT_svr", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "rf", "ETS", "FT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.9379,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.94) and high seasonality variance (0.70), with early horizon specialists (CWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_catboost) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_svr, CWT_svr). ARIMA dominates trend tracking while CWT_rf leads in seasonality capture. The FT_* models perform poorly across all metrics and should be excluded. Given the high disagreement and variance signals, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity; relative_spread_mean=0.094868 shows moderate disagreement. RMSE spread_ratio=0.94 (very high dispersion) favors top-k selection over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for phase-shift robustness with n_windows=3. Using small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 prevents overfitting with few windows. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific specialists."
}
raw.statistician: {"add_names": ["ridge_stacking_l210_topk5", "robust_median"], "remove_names": [], "params_overrides": {}, "rationale": "n_windows=3 is low (≤4), so add ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 from universe leaderboard (RMSE 4982.73 vs current best 5170.44). Add robust_median for SMAPE/MAPE improvement (SMAPE 0.04287, MAPE 0.0426 vs current best SMAPE 0.04527, MAPE 0.04507). Current set has good diversity with selection/weighted/DBA methods - no removals needed.", "changes": ["added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for low n_windows regularization", "added robust_median for SMAPE/MAPE improvement"], "when_good": "When n_windows >= 6, ridge_stacking becomes more viable per academic literature (Gaillard & Goude, 2015)"}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals extreme model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=0.94) and high seasonality variance (0.70), with early horizon specialists (CWT_rf, ONLY_DWT_catboost) differing from late horizon specialists (DWT_svr, CWT_svr). ARIMA dominates trend tracking while CWT_rf leads in seasonality capture. The FT_* models perform poorly across all metrics and should be excluded. Given the high disagreement and variance signals, DBA combination is recommended to leverage model diversity while mitigating individual weaknesses.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=10 indicates high model diversity; relative_spread_mean=0.094868 shows moderate disagreement. RMSE spread_ratio=0.94 (very high dispersion) favors top-k selection over full mean. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for phase-shift robustness with n_windows=3. Using small top_k=3 and shrinkage=0.25 prevents overfitting with few windows. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific specialists.

statistician.rationale: n_windows=3 is low (≤4), so add ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 from universe leaderboard (RMSE 4982.73 vs current best 5170.44). Add robust_median for SMAPE/MAPE improvement (SMAPE 0.04287, MAPE 0.0426 vs current best SMAPE 0.04527, MAPE 0.04507). Current set has good diversity with selection/weighted/DBA methods - no removals needed.
changes: added ridge_stacking_l210_topk5 for low n_windows regularization
statistician.when_good: When n_windows >= 6, ridge_stacking becomes more viable per academic literature (Gaillard & Goude, 2015)
